evidence_quotes.quote: Please try to prevent video game companies from doing what they want when they want because they do.
Rainbow Six Siege’s 39.99 product becomes completely free when the content appears to be the same in both cases, without that ‘exclusivity’ because it has paid for.
to force to buy ‘DLCs’ on more than one platform without crossave and then completely remove that content.

paragraphs.text: Please try to prevent video game companies from doing what they want when they want because they do. It is understandable that when an industry starts with a smaller audience and perhaps suffers from disadvantages for reasons beyond their control, they can go as far as doing what they do, but as early as 2025 we did not speak of little when we refer to video games, and it is clear that the current situation is due to the fact that it has not been involved in almost any effort to change how video game companies should act. My case specifically speaks of those video games which had a ‘x’ price that went to 0 and of video games which, despite having an ‘x’ price, eventually disappeared from the platform. In particular, I experienced the games displayed in the PDF: — Overwatch: Legendary edition (deleted) – Rocket League (now free) – Tom Clanc’s Rainbow Six Siege (now free) – Destiny 2: It is understandable that a company varies the price of its product according to the population and marketing strategies to keep the video game community alive, but it is frustrating when, for example, Rainbow Six Siege’s 39.99 product becomes completely free when the content appears to be the same in both cases, without that ‘exclusivity’ because it has paid for. It is true that ‘donated’ objects within the game that are nothing more than aesthetic. The same applies to Rocket League. The only ‘compensation’ was purely aesthetic objects, many of which were already repeated and could be obtained by the player in advance with microtransactions and level rewards. And what about the content? Exactly the same by paying 0 than 19.99. It is clear that this is something that needs to be taken more seriously, as such “compensations” actually suggest that even the video game company itself understands the problem of making a game entirely free of charge from one day to the next. Although this is a difference in my case for several years, this may be even more frustrating for players who made their purchase in the year preceding this change taken by the companies. And on the situation of Overwatch and Destiny 2, I think it is clear that it is ridiculous. Blizzard, creator of Overwatch (1) and Overwatch 2, decided to close Overwatch’s servers to make an equal game (Overwatch 2), but free of charge. And Destination 2? Unethical seems to me to force to buy ‘DLCs’ on more than one platform without crossave and then completely remove that content. Worse? In contrast to the rest, CERO provided compensation.
